response: Robert M. Fomon was a financier and served as the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of E. F. Hutton & Co. from 1970 to 1987. He also held the position of Governor of the New York Stock Exchange and was Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Pacific Stock Exchange. While he was not directly associated with the physical location of the New York Stock Exchange at 11 Wall Street, he was a key figure in the financial and regulatory landscape of the exchange. His role in the financial industry and his involvement with the New York Stock Exchange highlight his significant connection to the exchange's operations and governance.
